title: The boy who speaks with his hands
summary: Narrate becoming friends with a deaf boy on the street and learning a few words of sign language together.
topic: Becoming friends across a language barrier (sign language)
prompt: Write a short story (8–10 sentences) about making friends with someone who communicates in a different way (sign language, another language, drawing). Tell how you met, how you talk to each other, and what you learned. Use past simple to tell the story and present simple for what you do now.
hintStarters: A deaf boy lives in the flat below me, and one day we…
At first we could not talk, but…
Now, every time we meet, we…
sampleText: A deaf boy lives in the flat below me. His name is Marco, and he is about ten years old. One day, we met on the stairs and he waved at me with a big smile. At first we could not talk, because I did not know any sign language. So I pointed at things and he pointed back. He showed me the sign for 'water' and the sign for 'cat'. I copied him slowly, and he laughed when I made a mistake. We drew pictures on paper too, and that helped a lot. Over a few weeks, I learned ten signs from him. He learned three words from me by reading my lips. Marco is a very friendly boy, and he understands more than people think. Now, every time we meet, we say hello with our hands. I do not need many words to be his friend. He taught me that two people can talk without sound. The most important things in a friendship are not always loud.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: A deaf boy lives in the flat below me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một cậu bé khiếm thính sống ở căn hộ dưới tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he waved at me with a big smile
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cậu ấy vẫy tay với tôi cùng một nụ cười rộng
chúng tôi không thể nói chuyện
tôi chỉ vào các đồ vật

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he laughed when I made a mistake
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cậu ấy cười khi tôi làm sai
Chúng tôi vẽ tranh trên giấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: we say hello with our hands
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chúng tôi chào nhau bằng tay

sampleAnnotations.phrase: two people can talk without sound
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hai người có thể nói chuyện không cần âm thanh
không phải lúc nào cũng ồn ào

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for the story, present simple for now
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng quá khứ đơn để kể chuyện đã xảy ra ('we met', 'he waved', 'I learned') và hiện tại đơn cho hiện tại ('we say hello', 'he understands').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past simple to tell what happened ('we met', 'he waved', 'I learned') and the present simple for now ('we say hello', 'he understands').

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'could not' for past ability
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'could not' để nói về điều bạn không thể làm trong quá khứ: 'At first we could not talk.' Sau 'could not' luôn là động từ nguyên thể không 'to'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'could not' to talk about something you were unable to do in the past: 'At first we could not talk.' After 'could not' use the bare infinitive.

sampleTranslationVi: Một cậu bé khiếm thính sống ở căn hộ dưới tôi. Cậu ấy tên là Marco, và khoảng mười tuổi. Một ngày nọ, chúng tôi gặp nhau trên cầu thang và cậu vẫy tay với tôi cùng một nụ cười rộng. Lúc đầu chúng tôi không thể nói chuyện, vì tôi không biết ngôn ngữ ký hiệu nào cả. Vậy là tôi chỉ vào các đồ vật và cậu chỉ lại. Cậu chỉ cho tôi ký hiệu của từ 'nước' và ký hiệu của từ 'mèo'. Tôi bắt chước cậu thật chậm, và cậu cười khi tôi làm sai. Chúng tôi cũng vẽ tranh trên giấy, và điều đó giúp ích rất nhiều. Trong vài tuần, tôi học được mười ký hiệu từ cậu. Cậu học được ba từ từ tôi bằng cách đọc môi tôi. Marco là một cậu bé rất thân thiện, và cậu hiểu nhiều hơn người ta nghĩ. Bây giờ, mỗi lần gặp nhau, chúng tôi chào nhau bằng tay. Tôi không cần nhiều lời để làm bạn của cậu. Cậu đã dạy tôi rằng hai người có thể nói chuyện mà không cần âm thanh. Những điều quan trọng nhất trong một tình bạn không phải lúc nào cũng ồn ào.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence introduces Marco most naturally in the present?
options: A deaf boy live in the flat below me.
A deaf boy living in the flat below me.
A deaf boy lives in the flat below me.
A deaf boy lives in the flat below me he does.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A deaf boy' is singular, so the verb takes -s: 'lives'.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does a singular subject need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence tells how you met, in the past?
options: One day, we meet on the stairs and he waves at me with a big smile.
One day, we met on the stairs and he waved at me with a big smile he.
One day, we met on the stairs and he waving at me with a big smile.
One day, we met on the stairs and he waved at me with a big smile.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'met' and 'waved' are correct past simple forms for a finished event.
mcQuestions.hint: The meeting happened one day in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why you could not talk at first?
options: At first we could not talk, because I did not know any sign language.
At first we could not talked, because I did not knew any sign language.
At first we could not talk, because of I did not know any sign language.
At first we not could talk, because any sign language I did not know it.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'could not' use 'talk'; after 'did not' use 'know'; 'because' has a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Use the base verb after 'could not' and 'did not'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes how you communicated, in the past?
options: So I point at things and he points back.
So I pointed at things and he pointed back.
So I pointed at things and he point back.
So I pointed at things and back he pointing.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs are past simple 'pointed' for a finished event.
mcQuestions.hint: Both actions are in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes him teaching you a sign?
options: He show me the sign for 'water' and the sign for 'cat'.
He showed me the sign for 'water' and the sign for 'cat' he showed.
He showed me the sign for 'water' and the sign for 'cat'.
He showed me the sign for 'water' and 'cat' for the sign.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'showed' is the correct past simple, and the sentence is clear.
mcQuestions.hint: The action happened in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes him laughing at your mistake?
options: I copied him slow, and he laughed when I made a mistake.
I copied him slowly, and he laugh when I make a mistake.
I copied him slowly, and when a mistake I made he laughed it.
I copied him slowly, and he laughed when I made a mistake.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'copied', 'laughed', and 'made' are all past simple; 'slowly' is the correct adverb.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep all verbs in the past and use the adverb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence reports what you learned, in the past?
options: Over a few weeks, I learned ten signs from him.
Over a few weeks, I learn ten signs from him.
Over a few weeks, I learned ten sign from him.
Over a few weeks, ten signs from him I learned them.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'learned' is past simple, and 'signs' is plural.
mcQuestions.hint: It happened over past weeks, and the noun is plural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes Marco now, naturally?
options: Marco is a very friendly boy, and he understand more than people think.
Marco is a very friendly boy, and he understands more than people think.
Marco is a very friendly boy, and he understands more than people thinks.
Marco is a very friendly boy, and more than people think he understands it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'understands' agrees with 'he', and 'people think' is plural (no -s).
mcQuestions.hint: Match 'he' and plural 'people' to their verbs.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes your habit now?
options: Now, every time we meet, we says hello with our hands.
Now, every time we meet, with our hands we say hello it.
Now, every time we meet, we say hello with our hands.
Now, every time we meeting, we say hello with our hands.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'we meet' and 'we say' take no -s, and the order is natural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states the lesson he taught you?
options: He teached me that two people can talk without sound.
He taught me that two people can talks without sound.
He taught me that two people can talk without sound he taught.
He taught me that two people can talk without sound.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'taught' is the irregular past of 'teach', and after 'can' use the base verb 'talk'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'teach' has an irregular past, and 'can' takes a base verb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Can two people become close friends without sharing a spoken language, and what do you think you would learn from a friend who talks in a very different way?
